optimizer_switches.name: block_nested_loop
optimizer_switches.explanation: Buffers a batch of outer rows in a join buffer and scans the inner table once per batch instead of once per row. Inner still does a full/index/range scan — a missing index on the join column is the root cause.

warnings.text: Non-sargable join predicate: CONVERT(...) in t1.d = convert(concat('r',t2.y) using utf8mb4) — a function wrapped around the join column prevents index use.
Full table scan: t2 (1000 rows), t1 (3000 rows)
Block Nested-Loop (BNL) join buffer detected — uses join_buffer_size (Extra: 'Using join buffer (Block Nested Loop)', type ALL/index/range)

suggestions.action: Rewrite the join condition to compare the bare column on both sides (e.g. 'a.id = b.other_id' instead of 'CONCAT(a.id)=CONCAT(b.other_id)').
suggestions.why: wrapping a column in a function means the optimizer cannot use any index on that column — every row-pair is evaluated in the server layer, making the cost O(outer × inner). Dropping the function lets MySQL/MariaDB pick an index lookup or hash join with real selectivity, typically 100–1000× faster on mid-sized tables.

suggestions.action: Add indexes on filter/join columns to avoid full table scans
Add indexes to eliminate BNL full/index scans, or increase join_buffer_size. In MySQL 8.0.20+, set block_nested_loop=off to force hash join instead

executive_summary: Query scans 2 tables and joins via block nested-loop; returns 3,000 rows in 186 ms. Main finding: join predicate wraps the column in CONVERT() — no index can be used.

index_suggestions.reason: Full scan on t1 with filter on (d)

complexity.rationale: Unindexed nested loop: every outer row drives a full scan of the inner side. This is the classic O(n²)-class blow-up.
Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.
Full table scan: the storage engine returns every row; cost scales with the table size.
